ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Dula jigini ale Abrayam weeni ayen, wa powa Saara ka wa toa la. Gerar naawa Abimelek yaa tom ate ba ta Saara jam ate wa tuesi.
translated: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, "She is my sister," so Abimelek king of Gerar sent for Sarah and took her. Now Abimelek had not gone near her, and he asked, "Lord, will you destroy an innocent nation?"

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Wen yaa weeni ayen, <<Basi ate ŋanta tɔgatɔga ale tiisa a nyini tɛŋka zuk ate ba a yɔni yɔna a ta bie a va ba buuni ale ka diila.>> Ate ku nye dila.
translated: When the sun rises, "The dog says to the rooster that he should not crow loudly, that he should not wake the people in the house, that they are still sleeping, that they have not yet woken up." The dog does not know anything.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ba yaa zaani ŋandiinta wa niŋ, alege wa weeni ayen, <<M kan dɛ se ka m nin weeni m le ta diila. >> Laban yaa weeni ayen, <<Be weeni ti.>>
translated: They went to the river to bathe, the children of the village said, "I will go down to the river and I will see the fish." Then the village children said, "There is no fish."

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Yɔgyɔgla ba jam ta Josefi a cheŋ ka Ejipiti. Potifar Ejipiti denɔ waai ale jam ka Faaro nyaarɔma kpagi la a da wa a ta nyini Ismael demma waai ale ta wa a cheŋ dula la jigi.
translated: Joseph's brothers went to Egypt. Potiphar, an Egyptian, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had taken him there, and he became the property of Pharaoh's captain of the guard.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Abram le bo Keenan tɛŋka bena pi la ŋaaŋnyiŋ, wa powa Sarai yaa pai wa dɔkliewa Ejipiti denɔwa Aga a te wa chorɔwa ayen wa chim wa pok.
translated: Abram and his brother's son Lot separated, and Abram went with Sarai his wife and Lot his nephew to Egypt, but there was a famine in the land and they went down to Egypt to stay.
